sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Surf Air Mobility (SRFM) Quarterly Loss Of US$27 Million Tests Long Term Growth Narrative
feed.summary: Surf Air Mobility (SRFM) reported a Q3 FY 2025 revenue of US$29.2 million and a net loss of US$27.2 million, bringing its trailing twelve-month revenue to US$108.2 million against a total loss of US$72.4 million. The company's P/S ratio of 0.9x is higher than the industry average, but it continues to face substantial losses, negative shareholders' equity, and share dilution, with no profitability expected in the next three years despite forecasted revenue growth. Investors are debating whether potential future growth, driven by digital platforms and electrification, can outweigh the current financial challenges and consistent unprofitability.

feed.title: Notice to Long-Term Investors of Soleno Therapeutics, Inc. (SLNO): Grabar Law Office Investigates Claims on Your Behalf
feed.summary: Grabar Law Office is investigating Soleno Therapeutics, Inc. (SLNO) on behalf of long-term investors, alleging the company made materially false and misleading statements regarding the safety and commercial viability of its drug DCCR (VYKAT XR). The investigation highlights claims of undisclosed safety concerns in clinical trials, including excess fluid retention and a patient death, which led to a significant drop in stock price from over $90 to below $45 per share. Shareholders who purchased prior to March 26, 2025, are encouraged to contact the firm, and those who bought between March 26, 2025, and November 4, 2025, may be eligible for a securities fraud class action.
